text: Add my friend Marcin Kowalski. We went to university together, now he works at Google as a senior engineer. We chat on Signal, he lives in Zurich. Big fan of Rust and distributed systems.

content.text:
1|# Templates
 2|
 3|When the user asks to add, create, or capture a note, follow this process:
 4|
 5|1. Read the user's request and determine which template fits best.
 6|2. Pick the matching template from this folder.
 7|3. Fill in everything you can infer from the request and conversation context.
 8|4. For fields you cannot infer, ask the user — but only for essential ones. Use defaults from `mind.md` for the rest.
 9|5. Write the note to the correct folder based on the template's target section.
10|
11|## Available templates
12|
13|| Template       | File                | Target section       | When to use                                          |
14||----------------|---------------------|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------|
15|| Idea           | `idea.md`           | `Craft/Ideas`        | Quick thought, spark, unformed concept               |
16|| Knowledge      | `knowledge.md`      | `Craft/Knowledge/*`  | Learning note, research finding, technical concept   |
17|| Project        | `project.md`        | `Craft/Projects`     | Long-living entity: company, product, initiative     |
18|| Experiment     | `experiment.md`     | `Craft/Lab`          | Code prototype, POC, tool validation                 |
19|| Person         | `person.md`         | `World/People`       | Relationship: family, coworker, collaborator         |
20|| Source         | `source.md`         | `World/Sources`      | Information channel: podcast, blog, newsletter       |
21|| Event          | `event.md`          | `World/Events`       | Meeting, decision, conversation, time-bound record   |
22|| Service        | `service.md`        | `World/Tools`        | Tool, app, platform                                  |
23|| Shared         | `shared.md`         | `Craft/Shared`       | Published content: article, newsletter, workshop     |
24|
25|## Rules
26|
27|- Always apply frontmatter defaults from `mind.md`. Only set fields explicitly when overriding.
28|- Write as if the reader has zero prior context — no implicit references.
29|- Link to existing notes when mentioning people, projects, tools, or concepts that have their own note.
30|- Reuse existing tags. Check sibling notes before inventing new ones.
31|- Set `status: seed` for new notes unless the content is clearly mature.
32|- Never set `attention` unless there is a concrete handoff reason.
33|

content.text:
1|---
 2|title: "Linking Rules"
 3|description: "How notes should reference each other"
 4|status: evergreen
 5|publish: false
 6|tags: [system, rules]
 7|access.read: [agent]
 8|access.write: [human]
 9|---
10|
11|# Linking Rules
12|
13|## Wikilink format
14|
15|Use `[[path/to/note]]` for internal references. Paths are relative to `workspace/`.
16|
17|## When to link
18|
19|- Every mention of a person, tool, project, or concept that has its own note.
20|- Every note should link back to its parent index.
21|- Cross-area links are encouraged when the relationship is meaningful.
22|
23|## When not to link
24|
25|- Do not link common words that happen to match a note title.
26|- Do not create circular link chains with no content value.
27|
28|## External links
29|
30|- Use full URLs: `[label](https://example.com)`.
31|- Prefer permanent URLs over shortened ones — agents cannot infer content from opaque slugs.
32|- If an image is referenced, host it externally and embed as a URL, not a local file path.
33|

result.hint: File read complete. Checksum: 1e12267c18cf. To edit this file, use fs_write with this checksum. Reference lines by number for precise edits.

content.text:
1|---
 2|title: "Knowledge Base"
 3|description: "Root index of the personal knowledge base"
 4|status: evergreen
 5|publish: false
 6|access.read: all
 7|access.write: [human]
 8|---
 9|
10|# Knowledge Base
11|
12|Personal knowledge base organized into five areas. Agents read this file for orientation when creating or organizing notes.
13|
14|## Structure
15|
16|- **[[me/index]]** — identity, preferences. Human-authored, agents read for context.
17|- **[[world/index]]** — people, places, tools, sources, events. Shared between human and agents.
18|- **[[craft/index]]** — ideas, projects, knowledge, lab, shared content. The workspace for learning and creating.
19|- **[[ops/index]]** — workflows and operational output. Primary agent workspace. Active workflow: [[ops/daily-news/_info]].
20|- **[[system/index]]** — agent profiles, templates, rules. Human-owned, agent-readable.
21|
22|## Agents
23|
24|Four agents operate in this vault, defined in [[system/agents/]]:
25|
26|- **Alice** — orchestrator. Manages the vault, creates notes, delegates to specialists.
27|- **Ellie** — research. Searches the web, writes structured findings.
28|- **Tony** — writer. Assembles raw research into formatted output.
29|- **Rose** — delivery. Verifies content and sends via email.
30|
31|## Ownership
32|
33|| Folder    | Owner          | Purpose                                    |
34||-----------|----------------|--------------------------------------------|
35|| `me/`     | human          | Identity, preferences, personal process    |
36|| `world/`  | human + agent  | People, places, tools, sources             |
37|| `craft/`  | human + agent  | Knowledge, projects, experiments, sharing  |
38|| `ops/`    | agent          | Research, processes, operational output     |
39|| `system/` | human          | Agent config, templates, rules             |
40|
41|## Placement rules
42|
43|- Each note belongs to exactly one folder.
44|- Index files (`index.md`) serve as maps for their section — they link to children but contain no standalone content.
45|- Subfolder depth should not exceed 3 levels from workspace root.
46|- When unsure where a note belongs, place it in `craft/knowledge/` and tag it for review.
47|
48|## Conventions
49|
50|Linking rules are documented in [[system/rules/linking]].
51|Note templates live in [[system/templates/_index]].
52|

content.text:
1|# Template: Person
 2|
 3|Target: `World/People`
 4|Filename: `<firstname-lastname>.md`
 5|
 6|```yaml
 7|---
 8|title: "{{full_name}}"
 9|tags: [{{relationship_tags}}]
10|---
11|```
12|
13|## Sections
14|
15|### Who
16|How we know this person. Family, friend, coworker, collaborator, client — the relationship in one sentence.
17|
18|### Context
19|What we do together or how our paths cross. Role, company, shared projects, shared interests.
20|
21|### Communication
22|Preferred channels, timezone, any notes on style or scheduling. Useful for agents handling email or calendar.
23|
24|### Notes
25|Anything worth remembering that doesn't fit above. Conversations, shared history, things they care about.
26|

content.text: How we know this person. Family, friend, coworker, collaborator, client — the relationship in one sentence.

args.content:
---
title: "Marcin Kowalski"
tags: [person, friend]
---

## Who
Friend from university.

## Context
We went to university together. He now works at Google as a senior engineer. He is a big fan of Rust and distributed systems.

## Communication
We chat on Signal. He lives in Zurich.

## Notes
Related to [[world/index]].
